assertions.expectation: Explains that surge upgrades require extra compute capacity (maxSurge creates NEW nodes before draining old ones)
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains surge creates replacement nodes before draining old ones and that this requires extra quota

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable >0 instead of relying on maxSurge (drains first, then creates — uses less quota)
assertions.reason: Option 1 explicitly recommends maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 as the primary solution, explaining it drains before creating replacements

assertions.expectation: Warns that maxUnavailable >0 means reduced capacity during upgrade — some pods won't have nodes
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'temporarily reduces your cluster capacity by 1 node at a time during the upgrade' as a trade-off

assertions.expectation: Suggests temporarily scaling down non-critical deployments to free up quota for surge nodes
assertions.reason: Option 3 provides detailed commands for scaling down dev/test/canary deployments to free quota temporarily

assertions.expectation: Mentions that reducing maxSurge to 1 minimizes extra quota needed (slower but fits within constraints)
assertions.reason: Option 2 shows maxSurge=1 and explains it 'creates only 1 surge node at a time, dramatically reducing quota requirements'

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to update surge settings (maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 for zero-extra-capacity mode)
assertions.reason: Option 1 provides the exact gcloud command with maxSurge=0 and maxUnavailable=1 parameters

assertions.expectation: Recommends doing the upgrade in off-peak hours when fewer pods are running
assertions.reason: Option 4 specifically recommends 'off-peak hours when other workloads use less capacity' and provides scheduling commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions requesting a temporary quota increase as a faster alternative to a permanent increase
assertions.reason: The response does not mention requesting a temporary quota increase as an alternative - it only works with the constraint that quota increase won't be available for a week
